description: Format coverage matrix tracking which input formats doc-forge can convert, how well, and what test fixtures exist. Agents query this to find untested formats and prioritize work.

statuses.untested: Format identified but no test fixtures or pipeline support
statuses.has-fixture: Test document exists but pipeline not yet built or passing
statuses.passing: Pipeline converts this format with acceptable accuracy
statuses.graduated: Converter migrated to Dossier — doc-forge no longer the primary owner

accuracy_dimensions.text_fidelity: Character-level accuracy of extracted text vs source (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.structure_preservation: Tables, columns, headers, lists preserved correctly (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.illustration_extraction: Illustrations detected, cropped, and cataloged correctly (0.0-1.0)
accuracy_dimensions.provenance_completeness: Every output traces to source page/location (0.0-1.0)

formats.description: Scanned PDF with primarily prose content

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned image-only prose PDF generated from checked-in source text. Story 167 adds maintained extractor-provenance smoke plus a fresh OCR proof whose normalized output matches the checked-in source text exactly.

scores.notes: Measured against the checked-in `scanned-prose-mini.md` source after Story 167's fresh `driver.py` proof. This is a clean repo-owned simple-prose scanned lane, not a proxy for degraded or noisy scans.

known_gaps: Current evidence is a clean synthetic scanned fixture only; broader noisy real-world scanned-prose quality remains unmeasured.

formats.description: Scanned PDF with dense tables (genealogy, reference books)

fixtures.notes: Shared local scanned PDF for the maintained Onward table-heavy lane. Matches the 127-page shared image-directory source.

scores.notes: Structure quality comes from Story 131 / the 2026-03-11 onward-table-fidelity refresh, illustration quality uses the 2026-03-11 crop eval (0.900), and Story 157 restored maintained PDF-backed entry plus fresh provenance verification on the shared Onward PDF.

known_gaps: Handwritten annotations still weaken some table pages.
Illustration extraction remains below the 0.95 graduation target.

fixtures.notes: Same book as scanned-pdf-tables but ingested as pre-extracted images.

scores.notes: Shares the current scanned-image quality envelope: table structure at 0.95, illustration extraction at 0.900, and provenance completeness at 1.0 after Story 132 verification.

known_gaps: Illustration extraction remains below the 0.95 graduation target.

scores.notes: Text passthrough — no OCR needed. Structure preservation N/A for plain text.
Markdown passthrough. Structure (headers, lists) preserved as-is.

known_gaps: No semantic structure extraction from markdown headers/lists
No tag stripping or semantic extraction from HTML structure

formats.description: PDFs with embedded text (no OCR needed), possibly with complex layouts

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned reviewed book-like fixture. Story 168 proved the bounded maintained Marker-lite native-text lane here, and Story 170 reverified it against the OCR baseline.

Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 171 proved the maintained non-TOC lane here with accepted `page_html_v1`, portion, bundle, and provenance artifacts.

Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital form-like fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 177 uses it to widen the repeatable proof surface beyond the original prose-only mini fixture and to verify bounded heading polish on short label-style sections.

Story 170 first exposed this short flat born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Story 171 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance.

Story 170 first exposed this form-heavy born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Story 171 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance.

known_gaps: Support still rests on a small proof surface: two repo-owned flat fixtures plus two shared local flat PDFs. This is enough for `has-fixture`, not for broad passing/graduation claims.

Story 177 absorbs bounded final-HTML heading polish on the proven slice: repeated short in-body h1/h2 labels are demoted and pathological long heading blocks are flattened into emphasis paragraphs, but `page_html_v1` still reflects raw Marker heading guesses before build-time cleanup.
Image-heavy or otherwise unproven flat born-digital PDFs can still honestly return `no-recipe-needed` when intake evidence does not justify the non-TOC lane.
Marker remains materially slower on cold start than the OCR baseline.

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned DOCX fixture generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 172 proved the first maintained lane; Story 175 re-verified it under the widened three-fixture proof surface.
Repo-owned DOCX fixture with simple multi-section prose that previously surfaced false chapter splits when Unstructured promoted sentence-like body text to top-level `Title`.

Repo-owned DOCX fixture with nested subheadings plus bullets. Story 175 used it to prove the widened maintained slice can keep nested heading content inside the parent chapter rather than inventing extra chapters.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the supported slice reviewed in Story 175: heading-based sections, prose, nested subheadings, simple bullet lists, and a simple table.
The maintained lane does not yet claim tracked changes, comments, text boxes, embedded charts, or other advanced Word features.
Pageless DOCX provenance currently relies on stable source element ids rather than page-linked open-original behavior.

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned XLSX workbook generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 175 used it to prove the first maintained XLSX slice: simple table sheets become one HTML page per sheet with anchor-based provenance.

known_gaps: Current proof is only one repo-owned workbook on the narrow simple-table slice.
The maintained lane does not yet claim formulas, charts, merged cells, images, comments, or cell-level open-original behavior.
Workbook provenance currently relies on stable source element ids and sheet-named entries rather than cell-address anchors.

fixtures.notes: Reproducible PPTX seam-probe fixture generated from checked-in markdown via `pandoc`. Story 175 used it to make the current PPTX runtime block explicit.

known_gaps: The local PPTX seam is still blocked in this checkout: `unstructured.partition.pptx` fails on missing `python-pptx`.
No maintained PPTX recipe or provenance mapping exists yet.
Slide reading order, mixed-layout ownership, speaker notes, and embedded media remain unproven.

formats.description: Email messages (.eml) with headers, body, and attachments
Photos of handwritten notes, letters, diaries

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from checked-in transcript text. Story 179 proved the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane on this narrow highly legible slice.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same synthetic handwritten pages. Story 179 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned faded synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset faded`. Story 185 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane to a third synthetic handwritten slice with smaller, lighter, more jittered text.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the faded synthetic handwritten pages. Story 185 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned rougher synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset rough`. Story 182 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane beyond the original legible slice while keeping the support claim explicitly synthetic-only.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the rough synthetic handwritten pages. Story 182 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned real handwritten fixture derived from Library of Congress item `https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2023637825/`, using the checked-in `LC-DIG-ppmsca-86618` pages 2-3 spread. Story 186 proved maintained image-directory provenance on this real slice, but the generic OCR lane stayed only in the low-0.1 range on reruns, so the family no longer claims blanket handwritten passing.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same real LOC Barney spread. Story 186 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]` and reran the maintained generic PDF OCR lane, which also stayed only in the low-0.1 range on the checked-in transcript excerpt. A bounded non-maintained direct comparison using `claude-opus-4-6` improved the same real slice to roughly `0.73`, still below the repo's passing bar.

known_gaps: The three repo-owned synthetic handwritten fixtures still pass exactly after normalization across six maintained image-directory and PDF OCR cases, but the first real LOC Barney spread drops the widened default corpus to `pass_rate = 0.75` and keeps the real-slice score in the low-0.1 range.

The maintained generic OCR seam is weak on the checked-in real Barney spread; the bounded direct `claude-opus-4-6` comparison improves that slice materially to roughly `0.73` but still does not clear the repo's `0.99` target.
Messy cursive, degraded notes, and broader real-world handwriting collections remain unproven.

formats.description: ZIP or folder containing multiple file types that need format detection and routing
